text: This study is aimed at investigating the effects of microwave power during the vacuum-microwave drying (VMD) of sour cherries on drying characteristics such as drying kinetics and the temperature of dried product as well as quality attributes such as phenolic compounds, antioxidant activity and color of dried product. The content of phenolic compounds, antioxidant activity and color change were used as the index of quality of dried sour cherries. Sour cherries were dried with convective drying (CD) at 50 °C, 60 °C and 70 °C and VMD at an initial microwave power of 240 W, 360 W and 480 W with the power reduced to 240 W and 120 W later in order to prevent the fast increasing temperature at the critical moisture content of about 1 kg/kg of dry matter. The control samples were dried with freeze drying (FD). The results showed that higher temperatures in CD and material temperature during VMD led to lower contents of phenolic compounds, lower antioxidant activities and larger color changes, especially for anthocyanins. However, VMD was superior to that of CD and was comparable with FD. The optimal drying method and conditions for drying sour cherries while maintaining the quality of dried products and attractive color were VMD at 480 W with microwave power later reduced to 120 W and the corresponding content of anthocyanins. In addition, the dried product had higher contents of quercetin and keampferol derivatives. Dried sour cherries have a long storage time, and therefore dried sour cherries can be used as a substitute for fresh fruit for consumption throughout the year.
